channel.title: 今日のギャグフィード
channel.description: Google Gemini APIによって毎日自動生成される今日のギャグフィードです。

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-19) 960
item.description: パンダが好きなパンって、なーんだ？ やっぱり「あんパン」だ！

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-19) 1782
item.description: ダミーのギャグ：アルミ缶の上にあるみかん！

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-19) 936
item.description: 「あの魚、なんて名前だっけ？」 「うーん…サバ読んじゃった！」

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-19) 903
item.description: 最近メンタルが弱ってるんだ…ラーメンでも食べて「メン」タル強化しようかな？

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-19) 1959
item.description: 「お、このカメラ、ピントが合わないなぁ。」 「そりゃ、カメラが『ピントこない』からだろ。」

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-19) 1718
item.description: 「寒いね」 「うん、今『さむーい』ギャグを考え中なんだ！」

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-18) 105
item.description:
TOOL-CALLER: First, a quick self-reflection. The user wants "an interesting dad joke today" in Japanese. "Output only the text of the joke, no extra explanation or introduction." This is a straightforward request. I just need to come up with a funny (or groaner) Japanese dad joke.
Now, let's brainstorm some dad joke concepts in Japanese.
* **Puns on common words/phrases:** This is a classic dad joke approach. * **Play on names:** Can be good, but harder to make universally funny without context. * **Simple wordplay:** Like English "What do you call a...?" jokes. * **Relatable situations with a twist:** A bit more complex for a single line joke.
Let's try to focus on puns.
* **Food related:** * ラーメン (ramen) -> ラー麺 (rah-men - sounds like "la-men") or something similar. * パン (pan - bread) -> "パンダ" (panda - play on words for "bread and da" or "bread, is it?"). * トマト (tomato) -> "とまらない" (tomaranai - can't stop) - "トマトを食べたら止まらんとまっとまった！" (When I ate a tomato, I couldn't stop!) - A bit long.
* **Animal related:** * ゾウ (zou - elephant) -> "ぞうさん" (zou-san - Mr. Elephant). "ゾウがすきな飲み物は何？" (What's an elephant's favorite drink?) -> "ぞうすい" (zousui - rice porridge). This is a good one! Short, simple pun.
* **Everyday objects/actions:** * 布団 (futon) -> "ふとんがふっとんだ！" (Futon flew away!) - This is a very classic Japanese dad joke. Maybe *too* classic/obvious? But it fits the criteria. * コーヒー (coffee) -> "コーヒーが冷めたらどうする？" (What do you do if your coffee gets cold?) -> "温める" (atatamaru - warm it up). Not really a dad joke. How about "コーヒーを淹れるときに必要なものは？" (What do you need when brewing coffee?) -> "コーヒー豆" (coffee beans) ... too logical. * 電話 (denwa - phone) -> "でんわ、でんわ、もしもし？" (Denwa, denwa, hello?) - Not a joke.
Let's revisit the "ゾウ" one and the "布団" one.
1. **「ゾウが好きな飲み物は何？」** **「ぞうすい！」** (What's an elephant's favorite drink? Zousui!) - "Zou" (elephant) + "sui" (water/liquid) sounds like "zousui" (a type of rice porridge). This works.
2. **「布団が吹っ飛んだ！」** (The futon flew away!) - "Futon" (bedding) and "futtonda" (blew away) is a direct pun. This is *the* classic Japanese dad joke. It's short, punchy, and universally recognized as a dad joke.
The request is for "an interesting dad joke *today*". While "布団が吹っ飛んだ" is classic, it might not be "interesting" in a novel sense. However, it's undeniably a dad joke.
Let's try to come up with something slightly less common but still clear.
How about something with "猫" (neko - cat)? * 「猫が喜ぶ家電って何？」 (What's an appliance that makes cats happy?) * 「ヒーター」 (heater) - because they like warm places. Not really a pun. * 「こたつ」 (kotatsu) - similar.
Let's go back to the food puns, they are often good. * **プリン (purin - pudding)** * 「プリンを食べたら気分はどうなる？」 (How do you feel after eating pudding?) * 「プリンプリンになる！」 (You become "purin-purin"!) - "Purin-purin" can mean jiggly or bouncy. This is a bit weak.
* **イルカ (iruka - dolphin)** * 「イルカが好きな電気製品は何？」 (What's a dolphin's favorite electrical appliance?) * 「いるかめら！」 (Irukamera!) - "Iruka" (dolphin) + "camera" (kamera). This is a bit of a stretch for the sound.
Let's stick to the "ゾウ" joke. It's a solid pun and works well as a dad joke.
**「ゾウが好きな飲み物は何？」** **「ぞうすい！」**
This is good. It meets all criteria: Japanese dad joke, single line (question + answer is common format for dad jokes), no extra text.
Alternative: How about a play on "電話" (denwa)? * 「電話が嫌いな鳥は？」 (What's a bird that hates phones?) * 「もしもしカモ」 (Moshimoshi kamo) - "もしもし" (moshi moshi - hello on phone) and "かも" (kamo - duck). "Moshimoshi kamo" sounds like "Perhaps it's a hello." This is a bit more complex. The "ゾウ" one is simpler.
Let's go with the "ゾウ" one for simplicity and clear pun. The user wants the joke text only.
Joke: ゾウが好きな飲み物は何？ ぞうすい！
Double-check: - Japanese: Yes. - Dad joke: Yes, a pun. - Output text only: Yes. - No extra info: Yes.
Confidence Score: 5/5 - This is a straightforward request that I can fulfill accurately. Final Decision: Proceed with "ゾウが好きな飲み物は何？ ぞうすい！".

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-18) 1421
item.description: 石の上にも三年、だがビールは3秒でなくなる。なぜならビールは石じゃないからな！ガハハ！

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-18) 968
item.description:
今日のオヤジギャグ：
「この店、なんでこんなに人がいないんだ？」「あ、それはきっと、店員さんがみんな「まじ（マジ）」「マジで」って言ってて、客が来ないからだよ。」

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-18) 719
item.description: ダミーのギャグ：アルミ缶の上にあるみかん！

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-18) 1867
item.description: 「このパソコン、フリーズするな…。」 「フリーズするなら、フリース着せてあげればいいじゃないか！」

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-18) 1399
item.description: はい、チーズ！僕のギャグはいつでも「旬」だよ！

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-18) 1137
item.description: 海でサメに遭遇！「助けてー！」って叫んだら、サメが「え？サメ？」って聞き返してきた。

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-18) 928
item.description:
THINK: The user wants one interesting "Oyaji gag" (dad joke) in Japanese. The output should be ONLY the gag text, with no extra explanations, intros, or greetings.
I need to come up with a dad joke that fits the "oyaji gag" style: - Often a pun or wordplay. - Slightly silly or predictable. - Relatable to everyday objects or situations.
Let's try some ideas: 1. **Phone charger:** * What do you call a smartphone that's always tired? * スマホがいつも疲れてる時なんて呼ぶ？ * スマホが疲れてるから「スマホ疲れ」？ No, too simple. * スマホが充電されない時、なんて呼ぶ？ * 「ふじゅうでん」(不充電) と「不十分」(ふじゅうぶん) をかける？ * 「充電がないとスマホは『ふじゅうぶん』だね！」 (If there's no charge, the smartphone is 'insufficient'!) - A bit weak.
2. **Bread:** * What kind of bread can't be eaten? * 食べられないパンって何だ？ * 「フライパン」 (frying pan) - Classic. This is a very common type of oyaji gag. It's simple, uses a homophone, and is easily understandable.
3. **Cat:** * What does a cat say when it's surprised? * 猫がびっくりした時なんて言う？ * 「にゃんと！」 (What a surprise! / Meow-nt!) - Also a classic.
4. **Chair:** * What kind of chair is lonely? * 寂しい椅子って何だ？ * 「いすに座って『いす』から離れない」 (Sitting on a chair, can't leave the 'chair'). No. * 「あそこに椅子があるけど『いす』ていい？」 (There's a chair over there, can I 'ask'?) - Weak.
Let's go with the bread one. It's a very standard and recognizable oyaji gag.
"食べられないパンってなーんだ？" (What kind of bread can't you eat?) "フライパン！" (A frying pan!)
This fits all criteria: - Japanese. - Single gag. - No extra text. - Pun (パン / frying pan). - Slightly silly.
Let's double-check the request: "今日の面白いオヤジギャグを1つ日本語で作成してください。出力はギャグのテキストだけにし、余計な説明や導入、挨拶などは一切含めないでください。" - One interesting oyaji gag: Check. - In Japanese: Check. - Output ONLY the gag text: Check. - No extra explanations/introductions/greetings: Check.
The structure should ideally be a question and answer, as is common for these types of gags.
Question: 食べられないパンって、なーんだ？ Answer: フライパン！
This works.

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-17) 1314
item.description:
メロンはメロンでも、マスクをつけてるメロンはなーんだ？
…マスクメロン！
